specific_concerns: The quotable line 'The senior is awake to the trade-off being decided, asleep to the status performance happening on its surface' could be misread as 'be silent in meetings to look senior'. The engineering analog explicitly closes this misread in the falsifiability clause ('Most quiet engineers are simply unprepared... An engineer who is silent and not awake to anything is not the muni; she is just absent'), which travels with the line.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer does not flatten the verse. It preserves: (a) the technical Sanskrit term-pair saṁyamī / muniḥ verbatim; (b) the double-inversion structure (yā niśā... jāgarti / yasyāṁ jāgrati... niśā) verbatim mirrored; (c) Shankara's reading (paramārtha-sat vs vyāvahārika) operationalised as substrate-vs-surface; (d) the falsifiability clause that closes the be-silent-to-look-senior misread; (e) the counter-example (personnel decisions where surface IS substrate) honestly admitting the boundary of the verse's reading. Severity 1. Pass.
